name: United Quest Card
effective_cost: Medium: depends on the $200 travel credit and 2 free bags

verdict.text: Keep if you check two bags and can auto-use the $200 credit

earn_rates.category: Other travel (airfare, trains, transit, cruises, hotels, cars, rideshare, tolls)

earn_note: Chase markets '10x total miles' on United flights, but 6x of that is the base MileagePlus member earn on any United ticket; the card itself only adds 4x. The broader 2x travel/dining/streaming categories are genuinely useful, though, wider than Explorer's.

points.note: Same dynamically-priced MileagePlus currency as the Explorer and Club Infinite: no published award chart, no blackout dates, miles never expire. Quest adds a 10,000-mile anniversary award discount plus a baseline 10%+ discount on award tickets (15%+ with Premier status).

transfer_partners.highlight: MileagePlus can't be transferred out to other loyalty programs; miles earned here are locked into United.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: You can transfer INTO MileagePlus from Chase Ultimate Rewards (1:1, via a Sapphire or Ink Preferred card) and from Marriott Bonvoy, useful for topping up an award a few thousand miles short.

credits.name: United TravelBank Credit
credits.subtitle: $200 · auto-applied
credits.description: $200 in United TravelBank cash, deposited automatically at account opening and again each account anniversary; no spend threshold required.
tips: The easiest credit on this card: it lands automatically and TravelBank cash can be used toward almost any United ticket.

credits.name: Free Checked Bags
credits.subtitle: 1st + 2nd · you + 1 companion
credits.description: First and second checked bags free on United-operated flights for the primary cardholder and one companion on the same reservation.
tips: ~$35 + $45 each way per bag; a family checking two bags on one round trip clears a big chunk of the fee.
You don't need to buy the ticket with this card, just be on the reservation.

credits.name: Renowned Hotels Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $150 · prepaid stays
credits.description: Statement credits on prepaid stays booked through United's Renowned Hotels portal.
tips: Requires prepaying through the Renowned Hotels portal, not a direct hotel booking.

credits.name: Rideshare Credit
credits.subtitle: $8/mo (Jan–Nov) + $12 Dec
credits.description: Monthly U.S. rideshare statement credit after enrollment, with a bumped December credit.
tips: Enroll once, then a monthly rideshare habit captures it automatically.

credits.name: Avis/Budget Credit
credits.subtitle: $40 × 2 rentals
credits.description: Statement credit on up to two Avis or Budget rentals per year.
tips: Only useful if you already rent from Avis/Budget specifically.

credits.name: JSX Flight Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $150/yr
credits.description: Statement credit on bookings with JSX, a semi-private hop-on jet service in limited U.S. markets.
tips: Irrelevant unless JSX flies a route you'd actually use.

credits.name: Instacart Credit
credits.subtitle: $10 + $5/mo + 3-mo Instacart+
credits.description: Combined monthly Instacart statement credits, plus a 3-month complimentary Instacart+ trial (then 50% off the annual rate through 12/31/27).
tips: Monthly, no rollover; needs recurring Instacart orders to capture in full.

credits.name: Global Entry / TSA PreCheck / NEXUS Credit
credits.subtitle: $120 · every 4 years
credits.description: Statement credit for a Global Entry, TSA PreCheck, or NEXUS application fee, once every four years. Shown here amortized to an annual figure.
tips: Apply for Global Entry, not just TSA PreCheck; it includes TSA PreCheck and covers the same credit.

insurance.detail: Primary · up to $60,000 (secondary for NY residents)

protection_note: Identical protection package to the Explorer: solid free primary rental CDW and purchase protection, but trip cancellation ($1,500/person) and the 12-hour trip-delay trigger are both dated next to CSR's $10,000/person and 6-hour thresholds. Paying $200 more than Explorer buys bigger credits and a second free bag, not better insurance.
rental_note: Primary coverage up to $60,000 (secondary only for New York residents); decline the counter's CDW and pay with the card.

status_perks.name: Premier Qualifying Points
status_perks.note: 1,000 PQP bonus annually plus 1 PQP per $20 spent, capped at 18,000/yr, a real dent toward Premier Silver/Gold if you already fly United a lot.

status_perks.name: Global Economy Plus upgrades
status_perks.note: Two complimentary Economy Plus upgrades after $40,000 in annual card spend, a high bar for most cardholders.

status_perks.name: United Club access
status_perks.note: None. Unlike the cheaper Explorer's 2 annual one-time passes, Quest currently includes no lounge passes; step up to Club Infinite for real access.

services.name: Priority boarding
services.detail: Priority boarding on United-operated flights for the primary cardholder and companions on the reservation.

services.name: 25% back on inflight & Club purchases
services.detail: Uncapped statement credit rebate on food, beverages, and Wi-Fi onboard United-operated flights, plus premium drink purchases at United Clubs.

services.name: Award flight discounts
services.detail: 10,000-mile discount deposited every account anniversary, plus another 10,000-mile discount after $20,000 in annual spend.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Zero FX fees on international spend.

services.name: Visa Signature Concierge
services.detail: 24/7 concierge for reservations, tickets, and travel arrangements.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Free to add: no published annual fee for authorized users
10,000 bonus miles for adding one within the first 3 months (new accounts)
Earns MileagePlus miles (into the primary account)
Own PQP allotment or Economy Plus upgrades
Free checked bags only when traveling with the primary cardholder on the same reservation

additional_cards.note: Adding an authorized user is free and was worth 10,000 bonus miles during the current offer, but the second-bag and priority-boarding perks only apply when the AU is actually traveling with the primary cardholder; there's no separate PQP or upgrade allotment for them.

timeline.text: Fee $250 → $350; the flat travel credit became a $200 auto-applied TravelBank deposit, added a 1,000 PQP annual bonus, an annual 10,000-mile award discount, and Avis/Budget, JSX, and expanded Instacart credits.
United Club one-time passes removed from Quest as part of the same refresh that raised the fee; lounge access is now Explorer (2 passes) or Club Infinite (unlimited) only.
